text: output of "gittoc log" is missing information on actor.
yeah, I think I'd care about the actor in commit-messages *on the ticket branch*.

Per user's note: add actor to commit messages on the gittoc branch. commit_if_needed() currently takes a plain message string. Could append ' (by <actor>)' or similar. Need to thread actor through to commit_if_needed — some callers already have actor, others infer it. Small change, mostly plumbing.
maybe we should even consider having a sensible summary message in all commits to the gittoc branch.

Broadening scope: all commit messages on the gittoc branch should include actor. Currently messages are like 'Add note to T-42' — should become 'Add note to T-42 (dpiegdon)' or 'Claim T-42 for claude'. Most callers already have actor context. The commit messages themselves are already descriptive, so this is mainly about attribution.

Actor attribution matters more now than before because the intended peer users are no longer just one agent and one human. With Codex, Claude, and humans all expected to touch the tracker, git-log output without actor information will age poorly as soon as collaboration gets denser.
